Genentech company overview mission focus.query: Genentech company overview mission focus areas 2024 2025
Genentech company overview mission focus.answer: Genentech focuses on developing innovative treatments for serious diseases, aiming to improve patient health and quality of life. Their mission includes addressing health disparities and promoting inclusivity. Genentech commits to socially, environmentally, and economically responsible practices.

results.title: Inclusion & Belonging - Genentech
results.content: Their mission is to provide support and visibility for Genentech employees sharing of Black heritage by leveraging the distinct strengths and abilities of members that will promote inclusiveness internally throughout Genentech and externally in our communities. APAC^2 is on a mission to enrich diversity, support health education in our communities, elevate cultural awareness and enhance personal and professional development to the Genentech community and its partners through inclusion, outreach efforts, and cultural events. The mission of this group is to develop and maintain a strong LGBTQIA: Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, Transgender, Queer, Questioning, Intersexed and Allies – community at Genentech that fosters inclusion of all people. SANG is focused on promoting awareness of the South Asian culture on the South San Francisco campus, providing a forum to enhance career growth and actively participating in Genentech’s diversity initiatives. Vision, Integrity, Development, Action (VIDA) is committed to sharing Latinx culture, and our commitment to life (Vida), promoting professional success and recruiting diverse talent to Genentech.

results.title: Our Promise - Genentech
results.content: # OUR PROMISE. # What is Our Promise? The drive to be bold and courageous changemakers – not only in science but for society – unites everyone working here today. That's why we created Our Promise — a commitment to ourselves, each other, patients, and society. Help us deliver on Our Promise and advance health for the greater good. # Our Promise in Action. Genentech pioneered the biotechnology industry and revolutionized treatment for some of the most complex health problems. # Our Promise in the Future. We aim to continue delivering treatments to improve countless lives for the next 50 years and beyond. Together, we can improve all patients' health and quality of life and be a catalyst for driving positive change in society. We remain dedicated to creating a culture of inclusivity, integrity, and creativity – empowering each other to grow and challenging our industry to be better.

results.title: [PDF] Genentech is committed to addressing health care disparities
results.content: 2 That is why Genentech is: Addressing Care Gaps •	Nonmedical factors (housing, education, affordability, health literacy) affect health outcomes and can significantly vary by ZIP code2,3 •	Health care and community organizations have highlighted addressing health inequities as an important priority4-6 Advancing Inclusive Research •	Only 20% to 30% of US clinical trial participants are not White7,8 •	~86% of available genomic material is of European ancestry9 Health care access and outcomes can differ by ZIP code.2,3* US=United States. https://www.kff.org/racial-equity-and-health-policy/issue-brief/ disparities-in-health-and-health-care-5-key-question-and-answers/ 3. https://diabetes.org/healthequitynow/health-equity-programs Examples of Our Work Improving Patient Access and Affordability Over the past 28 years, Genentech has helped more than 3.7 million people get the medicines they need through patient assistance programs.11 Scan the QR code to learn more about how we help patients.

results.title: Good - Genentech
results.content: We are committed to advancing science for the greater good. As we tackle some of the world’s most complex health challenges, we do so knowing that inclusion drives innovation, that we must develop and deliver medicines in an environmentally, economically and socially responsible way, and that the ways we give back — through our charitable giving and employee time and talent — can have a powerful impact on our communities. As we tackle some of the world’s most complex health challenges, we do so knowing that inclusion drives innovation, that we must develop and deliver medicines in an environmentally, economically and socially responsible way, and that the ways we give back — through our charitable giving and employee time and talent — can have a powerful impact on our communities. The Resilience Effect is our flagship philanthropic initiative committed to addressing the negative effects of childhood adversity on the health and well-being of Bay Area children and families. One of our newer buildings was designed according to four pillars: sustainability, wellbeing, community and innovation.

Genentech machine learning AI tech stack.query: Genentech machine learning AI tech stack infrastructure
Genentech machine learning AI tech stack.answer: Genentech uses AI powered by strategic collaboration with NVIDIA, leveraging GPU-accelerated models for drug discovery. They employ MLOps for infrastructure and have hired machine learning engineers for their AI initiatives.

results.title: AI Fuels Genentech's R&D Ecosystem
results.content: The foundation of Genentech's R&D strategy centers on the “lab in a loop,” where data from the lab and clinic feed AI models designed by our researchers to identify trends, make predictions, propose new experiments, and generate new molecular designs. Our strategic collaboration with NVIDIA, which began in 2023, accelerated our ability to rapidly predict the structure of molecules, reduce training time for our biological AI models, and streamline image analysis – compressing some of the slowest steps of drug discovery, enhancing our ability to discover molecules, and boosting the efficiency of our “lab in a loop.” For example, our Equifold model, trained in part with the NVIDIA BioNeMo platform, was our first model to predict the dynamic conformational ensembles of antibodies, thereby turning a GPU-hours-long calculation of surface properties into seconds and enabling such calculations to scale to early research.

results.title: Understanding the GenAI Tech Stack — Part 1: MLOps
results.content: MLOps is an expansive category of businesses that encompass a variety of organizational infrastructure activities with a critical role in the tech stack.

Genentech funding stage Series valuation.answer: Genentech raised $60 billion in a public funding round in 2024, achieving a valuation of $65 billion. The funding was led by Roche. Genentech's latest known valuation is $65 billion.

results.content: # Genentech - Company Profile. ## Genentech - About the company. Genentech is an acquired company based in San Francisco (United States), founded in 1976byRobert A Swanson and Herbert W Boyer. Genentech has raised $72.5M in funding, with last known valuation of  $\*\*\*\*\*. ## Genentech's funding and investors. Here is the list of recent funding rounds of Genentech:. | 17th | Logo for Genentech  Genentech 1976, San Francisco (United States), Acquired | R&D company focused on manufacturing drugs for multiple therapeutic areas | $72.5M | Ignite 500, Mayfield  & 4 others | 67/100 |. Genentech has made 14 investments in companies including Equify Health and Pieris Pharmaceuticals. View complete company profile of Genentech. Genentech ranks 17th amongst 1159 active competitors, of which 64 are funded. Genentech'slatest funding was a  $\*\*\*\*\* Grant (prize money) round on Sep 22, 2023, with participation from 1 investor. Genentech'stop competitors includeAmgen, Merck and AbbVie. Among them, Johnson & Johnson secured the most recent funding round in February, 2025.

results.title: [PDF] 2025 SSF Genentech Civic Fund RFP
results.content: In order to be considered for funding, we ask that you demonstrate that your program or event meets the following criteria: ●​ SSF-based organizations and/or programs or events that primarily serves SSF residents or enhances and strengthens the SSF community (defined as 30%+ of program services/hours serving SSF residents, or 30%+ event attendees live or work in SSF ) OR Programs or events that serve San Mateo County residents or enhances and strengthens the communities of San Mateo County ●​ Program or event takes place within South San Francisco or the larger San Mateo County ●​ Request is within the $5K minimum ~ $25K maximum Funding Requirements & Restrictions Your organization must be recognized by the IRS as a tax exempt, public charity under sections 501(c)(3) and 501(c)(6) of the Internal Revenue Code and be located in the United States; or be a US governmental organization (such as public schools, public colleges and universities, public hospitals and federally recognized Indian tribal governments).

Genentech culture remote work life balan.query: Genentech culture remote work life balance
Genentech culture remote work life balan.answer: Genentech promotes a culture that values work-life balance, offering flexible remote work options and benefits. Employees appreciate the company's efforts to maintain a healthy work environment. Genentech's commitment to balance is reflected in its employee reviews and policies.
